arts.ttl: 'Not right' - Enzo Maresca explains why he cancelled Chelsea day off ahead of Tottenham - football.london
arts.sum: Chelsea head coach Enzo Maresca emphasized the importance of standards after he cancelled a scheduled day off due to what he deemed as a poor showing in training. With a reduced squad during the international break, Maresca was left unhappy with the performance of some members of the squad during an informal game against a Chelsea youth team last Wednesday.

arts.ttl: Tottenham have been given clear route to signing – Enquiries arrived from England
arts.sum: Over the past few days, Tottenham Hotspur have been linked with a move for Genoa defender Koni de Winter. There was an indication that Ange Postecoglou’s side are leading the race for the versatile centre-back, who can also play at right-back. arts.ttl: Chelsea scouts could be set for Sunday transfer mission – Worry growing over transfer
arts.sum: Last week, Chelsea emerged as a target for Torino goalkeeper Vanja Milinkovic-Savic. While Manchester City have also been mentioned as a suitor for the Serbian, the report last week suggested that Enzo Maresca is keeping a close eye on Milinkovic-Savic, who has a release clause of €20m in the summer. arts.sum: But regardless of whether Postecoglou leads Spurs to Europa League glory, his chances of remaining at the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium will have slightly increased at Bournemouth's demise. The Tottenham Hotspur boss continues to face an uncertain future in North London as Spurs' season slides towards a crucial climax.

arts.ttl: Just as the football fan who assaulted me escapes charges, Spurs are hosting Chris Brown | Eve De Haan
arts.sum: While my assaulter can continue to attend games undeterred, Tottenham announce a convicted abuser as a performer. Are the club’s actions matching their words when it comes to supporting female fans? A few months ago I was assaulted on an overground train by a Brentford fan after a home win at the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. The British Transport Police were rapid in their response, unsuccessfully but immediately halting a Victoria line train to find him, before arresting him the next week on his own way to his team’s home match. Over a few months of back and forth updates with the BTP the case was passed to the Crown Prosecution Service. This Saturday at a sunny pub with my dad, among chatter about Tottenham’s seasons’ woes while an FA Cup tie played out on the TV screens I got an unexpected call from BTP for a final update. The CPS had decided there was not enough evidence to secure a conviction against the individual. Continue reading...
